free_text: New method for production of 155Tb via 155Dy by
irradiation of natGd by medium energy alpha particles
Gadolinium oxide targets of natural isotopic
Dy < 0.01%, Tb <0.07%) were used. The average
thickness of each target was determined by weighing
as 8-12 mu-m. The surface of each target was
additionally covered with a thin 8.3 mu-m aluminum
foil in order to prevent them from sticking together
The cross sections were measured by the
A target assembly was irradiated for 50 min,
and an average current was of 0.1 mu-A. For each
target, 3-4 measurements were made within 8 days
The activity of the nuclear reaction products
was measured on a gamma-ray spectrometer equipped with
ORTEC GEM 35P4 high purity germanium detector with an
energy resolution of 850 eV for the 122 keV line and
1.8 keV for the 1.33 MeV line. The distance from the
detector was chosen so that the dead time did not
Comparison with data from TENDL-21 library was
The uncertainty of the alpha particle energy on the
target ranged 1.4-6.8 MeV, depending on the position
of the target in the stack.
Entry was approved by Dr. A.N.Moiseeva
